questions: Where do outside-repository references live in tracked/published surfaces, by class?
Which surfaces are hash-bound and what does editing them actually break?
What remediation conventions keep behavior and required attribution intact?

findings.claim: ~237 instances: ~87 prose-doc, ~51 in 23 sealed ledger entry bodies, 7 gate-artifact sessions (sidecar-bound), 7 tracked intent-lock records, inherited dist copies, plus published GitHub items
findings.citation: mechanical sweep 2026-07-04 (terms held as private reasoning per the doctrine)

findings.claim: Ledger body edits keep chain verification green (chain math consumes recorded hash fields; latest-only plan-binding CI check); gate-artifact edits require sidecar payload_sha256 regeneration
findings.citation: qor/scripts/ledger_hash.py chain_hash/verify; qor/reliability/seal_entry_check.py check_latest; qor/scripts/gate_provenance.py verify_sidecar

findings.claim: A tracked lint cannot carry the private denylist; structural patterns + optional gitignored operator-local terms file
Scrub sealed surfaces in place + one disclosure REMEDIATION entry; GitHub sweep covers all open + today-created items with historical follow-on worklist
